notes: The set forms a coherent visual spine on the locked 'deliver' thread: it opens on the specific Psalm 22:7 mockery (shot-out lip, shaken head, jabbing finger), establishes the prophecy with David recording the taunt (scenes 2,3,12), lands the Matthew fulfilment at the cross (scenes 4,5,13), then turns up to the sovereign, restrained Christ (scenes 8,9,14) and closes on willing love (10,11). No theology drift: the conviction scenes deliberately show withheld power (Matt 26:53) and a relaxed, open nailed hand, guarding against any 'He couldn't come down' reading — matching the narration's explicit denial of impotence. Every narration beat is carried by at least one strong scene; hook->climax->close (shaking heads -> sovereign cross -> 'He chose to stay') is a clean spine. Hero #11 is the gospel-pivot cross, so the cut lands on Christ.
